articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: IMF warns South Africa of recession
more.description: JOHANNESBURG – The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned that the global economy faces recession if the conflict in the Middle East continues—with energy-importing countries like South Africa at even greater risk. In a report published last week, IMF economists warned that a longer or broader conflict could significantly weaken growth and destabilise financial markets. more.title: Two Marches, One Nation: What Independence Day in London Tells Us About Zimbabwe
more.description: On Saturday, two groups of Zimbabweans marched through the same streets of central London, within hours of each other, to mark the same Independence Day and arrived at completely opposite conclusions about what that independence means. By Gift Kugara – Mawire The first march, organised by the Citizens Protest Movement, gathered outside Zimbabwe House on […]The post Two Marches, One Nation: What Independence Day in London Tells Us About Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe at 46 - Between Promise and Reality
more.description: [allAfrica] Zimbabwe is marking 46 years of independence (1980-2026)--a journey that began on 18 April 1980, when the country attained majority rule and stepped into a future defined by self-determination and the promise of shared prosperity.

more.title: Highlanders coach Mwaruwari owed 3 months’ salary as Chivayo payment chain breaks down
more.description: BULAWAYO – Highlanders coach Benjani Mwaruwari has not been paid for three months, ZimLive can reveal, after a payment chain running through millionaire sponsor Wicknell Chivayo’s associates broke down. The 47-year-old, who took charge at the Bulawayo club in January, has received just one salary payment since his appointment. Several other contractual obligations remain unmet, […]The post Highlanders coach Mwaruwari owed 3 months’ salary as Chivayo payment chain breaks down appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Obert Mpofu’s fall signals Zanu-PF tensions
more.description: Nine years ago, Obert Mpofu stood beaming beside Emmerson Mnangagwa, having played a pivotal role in the dramatic events that ushered in a new political era.
